title: Life expectancy gap for Black Bostonians is growing, health officials warn
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Life expectancy for Black Bostonians fell to 76.2 years by 2024, trailing the city's overall 82.2 years.
A Boston health report highlights unintentional overdose, cancer, and cardiometabolic disease as key causes of early Black mortality.
Boston invests $1 million in health initiatives for Black men and partners with Dana-Farber Cancer Institute.

title: Former Celtics center Luke Kornet calls out Hawks over ‘Magic City’ promotion
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Former Celtics center Luke Kornet criticized the Atlanta Hawks' 'Magic City Night' promotion celebrating a well-known strip club.
Kornet urged the NBA and Hawks to cancel the event, citing concerns over objectification and mistreatment of women.
The Hawks' promotion includes a performance by rapper T.I. and features Magic City's famous lemon pepper wings.

title: Married couple from R.I. identified as victims in fatal Swansea crash
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Carlolyn Carcasi, 54, and James Carcasi, 53, from Bristol, R.I., died in a Swansea crash February 27.
The car crash occurred at the intersection of Route 136 and Route 6; their Subaru was engulfed in flames.
Demitri Sousa, 28, allegedly caused the crash and shot a man in R.I. hours earlier, police say.

title: Steve Kerr apologizes for comments about Kristaps Porzingis’ lingering illness
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Kristaps Porzingis has been limited to 18 games this season due to continued illness affecting his performance.
Steve Kerr apologized for discussing Kristaps Porzingis' medical condition, acknowledging his comments were a mistake.
Porzingis revealed he is dealing with postural orthostatic tachycardia syndrome, impacting his play since March 2025.

title: Taking stock of Bruins’ top trade chips entering the NHL deadline
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Boston Bruins GM Don Sweeney considers trading assets like draft picks and players before the NHL trade deadline.
Key trade assets include defenseman Mason Lohrei and forward Viktor Arvidsson, both potential candidates for team improvements.
The Bruins hold multiple first-round draft picks, including a valuable 2026 pick from Toronto, highly regarded as a trade asset.

title: NFL insider: Maxx Crosby trade could happen ‘this week’
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Patriots among multiple teams interested in Raiders' Maxx Crosby as trade discussions intensify.
Insider reports suggest a potential Maxx Crosby trade could materialize by the end of the week.
Raiders show reluctance to formally shop Crosby despite receiving numerous inquiries from NFL teams.

title: Quincy man sentenced in fatal St. Patrick’s Day assault in downtown Boston
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Sanusi Sadiq sentenced to 3-6 years for manslaughter in the 2023 St. Patrick's Day assault in Boston.
Barry Whelan died from a brain injury after a confrontation with Sadiq, ruled a homicide by medical examiners.
Sadiq and Whelan's argument was reportedly racial, escalating to Sadiq striking Whelan who fell and sustained fatal injuries.

title: Bill Simmons seems convinced that ‘all signs are pointing to’ this date for Jayson Tatum’s return
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Jayson Tatum aims to return to the Celtics in front of a home crowd, possibly this Friday against the Mavericks.
Bill Simmons believes all signs indicate Tatum will make his season debut Friday at TD Garden against Dallas.
The Celtics and Mavericks game in Boston will be nationally televised, featuring Maine native Cooper Flagg if he is fit to play.

title: Should teens be allowed to carry handguns in Massachusetts?
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Massachusetts Supreme Judicial Court to hear case on legality of handgun possession for those under 21.
Gun-rights advocates challenge state's age restriction citing Second Amendment protections after 2022 Supreme Court decision.
Case initiated by appeal of Mikai Thomson, convicted at 20 for illegal firearm possession in 2021.

title: 5 takeaways from Don Sweeney’s Bruins press conference before trade deadline
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Don Sweeney remains open to trades before the Bruins' trade deadline but won't pursue short-term rentals, focusing on long-term growth.
The Bruins seek impact players with long-term contracts, considering options like Robert Thomas and Owen Tippett for potential acquisitions.
Sweeney emphasizes the need to maintain depth, not rushing outgoing trades while exploring strategic options to strengthen the team.
